questionText: Lis Sørensen represented Denmark in the Eurovision Song Contest twice. What year was her first Eurovision entry?
funFact: Lis Sørensen became a Danish pop legend through her powerful voice and emotional rock ballads that dominated Danish radio for decades!

questionText: Heilung is a Danish folk/neo-folk group known for their ancient Norse aesthetic. What does their name mean?
funFact: Heilung performs with reconstructed Viking Age instruments and has become a global sensation for their immersive, ritualistic live performances!

questionText: Junior Senior was a Danish duo that had a massive international hit in 2003. What was the name of that hit single that became a global party anthem?
funFact: Junior Senior's 'Move Your Feet' became a viral sensation in 2003, dominating dance floors worldwide and putting Denmark on the pop-dance map!

questionText: Pretty Maids is a Danish heavy metal band that's been performing since 1982. How many studio albums have they released as of 2023?
funFact: Pretty Maids remains one of Denmark's most consistent metal exports, touring globally and maintaining a loyal fanbase for over 40 years!

questionText: MØ, the Danish pop star, collaborated with which major international DJ on the 2015 hit 'Lean On'?
funFact: MØ's real name is Karen Marie Ørsted, and 'Lean On' became Spotify's most-streamed song ever at the time!

questionText: Mercyful Fate, Denmark's legendary metal band, was fronted by which iconic vocalist known for his operatic style?
funFact: King Diamond's black and white face paint became an instantly recognizable symbol in metal culture worldwide!

questionText: Alphabeat had a viral international hit in 2007. What was the title of this catchy pop-dance track that became a global phenomenon?
funFact: Alphabeat's 'Fascination' music video featured the whole band in neon colors and became a meme before memes were mainstream!

questionText: Veto is a Danish rock band that emerged in the 2000s indie scene. Which famous Danish music festival have they headlined multiple times as one of the festival's flagship acts?
funFact: Veto's chaotic, energetic live shows at Roskilde Festival are legendary — they once played while crowd-surfing themselves!

questionText: Efterklang, the ethereal indie band known for their experimental soundscapes, hails from which Danish city?
funFact: Efterklang's album 'Magic Chairs' was recorded in a specially-built wooden structure in Iceland!

questionText: Safri Duo's massive 2000 hit 'Played-A-Live' sampled a classical piece. Which composer wrote the original?
funFact: The track became a global phenomenon and is still played at sporting events worldwide — pure Danish electronic genius meets 18th-century classical!

questionText: Røde Mor was one of Denmark's most important rock bands of the 1970s. Their name translates to what in English?
funFact: Røde Mor was so politically engaged that their music became the soundtrack to the Danish left-wing movement — they were basically Scandinavia's answer to The Rolling Stones' activism!

questionText: Kölsch, Denmark's electronic music ambassador, has been a resident DJ at which world-famous Ibiza nightclub since 2012?
funFact: Kölsch's real name is Jens Kölschbach, and he's put Denmark firmly on the global electronic music map — proving Scandinavian minimalism works perfectly on the dancefloor!

questionText: Lukas Graham's massive 2015 hit '7 Years' topped charts worldwide. What is the lead singer's real first name?
funFact: The song '7 Years' spent 32 weeks in the US Billboard Hot 100 top 10 — the longest-charting Danish song ever!

questionText: Volbeat, Denmark's groove metal legends, was founded by which Danish musician who previously played in another metal band?
funFact: Michael Poulsen is also an accomplished songwriter for other Danish bands and has won multiple Danish Music Awards!

questionText: Mew, the progressive rock band from Denmark, has been active since 1994. Which Danish city are they from?
funFact: Mew's album 'And the Glass Handed Kites' was nominated for a Grammy, making them one of Denmark's most critically acclaimed rock exports!

questionText: Tommy Seebach was a Danish pop icon in the 1970s-80s. He represented Denmark at Eurovision how many times?
funFact: Tommy Seebach's disco-pop style made him a household name in Denmark and Scandinavia during the disco era!

questionText: Trentemøller, Denmark's electronic music pioneer, is known for his lush, atmospheric productions. Which Copenhagen-based record label did he co-found?
funFact: Trentemøller's 'Harbour Lights' became an underground classic and established Denmark as a serious electronic music hub!

questionText: TV-2, one of Denmark's longest-running rock/pop bands, achieved massive success in which decade with hits like 'Tænk hvis' and 'Pak en picnic'?
funFact: TV-2 sold millions of albums across Scandinavia and became one of Denmark's most beloved domestic rock bands of all time!

questionText: Lukas Graham's biggest international hit '7 Years' topped charts worldwide. In which year was this mega-hit released?
funFact: '7 Years' spent 76 weeks on the Billboard Hot 100 — one of the longest chart runs in history!

questionText: King Diamond, Denmark's legendary metal vocalist, is known for his operatic voice and shock rock performances. What is his real name?
funFact: King Diamond's signature pale makeup and leather outfit inspired generations of metal musicians worldwide!

questionText: Carl Nielsen, one of Denmark's greatest classical composers, served as conductor of which prestigious Danish institution for many years?
funFact: Carl Nielsen's 'Maskarade' opera is considered one of the finest Danish operas ever written and is still performed worldwide!

questionText: Aqua, the 1990s Eurodance sensation behind the global hit 'Barbie Girl,' featured which Danish female vocalist alongside René Dif?
funFact: Lene Nystrøm and René Dif have reunited Aqua multiple times — their fans simply won't let the group retire!

questionText: Mads Langer, the Danish singer-songwriter, topped the Danish singles chart with which emotional ballad in 2009?
funFact: Mads Langer's 'Where You Go' became so popular in Denmark that it's been covered by multiple Danish artists as a tribute!

questionText: The Minds of 99, Danish rock revivalists, took their name from which historical reference?
funFact: The Minds of 99 named themselves as a statement about the end of the millennium — they wanted to capture that moment in Danish rock history!

questionText: Poul Krebs, Denmark's rock legend, famously performed at which major Scandinavian rock festival annually since the 1980s, making him a festival icon?
funFact: Poul Krebs has performed at Roskilde Festival so many times that he's considered part of the festival's DNA!

questionText: Gilli, the Danish hip-hop sensation, released his breakthrough hit 'Øjet på Mig' featuring which style that blended trap and Danish storytelling?
correctAnswer: Trap-influenced hip-hop with poetic Danish lyrics
options: Trap-influenced hip-hop with poetic Danish lyrics

questionText: Tina Dickow, the Danish folk-pop legend, is known for her emotional ballads and served as a coach on which Scandinavian TV talent show?

questionText: Artillery, Denmark's thrash metal legends, released their seminal album 'By Inheritance' in which decade?
funFact: Artillery is considered one of the 'big four' of Scandinavian thrash metal and has influenced countless European metal bands since the late 80s!

questionText: Rasmus Seebach's massive hit 'Engel' topped the Danish charts in which year?
funFact: 'Engel' became one of the best-selling Danish pop songs of all time and was used in countless Danish weddings!

questionText: Michael Learns to Rock, the Danish pop group, achieved their biggest international success in which decade?
funFact: Michael Learns to Rock's 'Take Me to Your Heart' became one of the most beloved karaoke songs in Asia, especially in China!

questionText: Cartoons, the Danish house/pop group, achieved massive success with which catchy novelty song in 1997?
funFact: 'Witch Doctor' became a global earworm and topped charts worldwide, proving that sometimes the silliest songs become timeless classics!

questionText: Suspekt, Denmark's legendary hip-hop group, released their landmark debut album 'Suspekt' in which year?
funFact: Suspekt's album 'Suspekt' is considered the birth of Danish hip-hop as we know it today!

questionText: Agnes Obel, the Danish indie-pop sensation, is known for which signature musical instrument in her live performances?
funFact: Agnes Obel's haunting piano melodies have been featured in major TV dramas across Europe and the US!

questionText: Vagn Holmboe, the legendary Danish classical composer, is best known for his series of how many symphonies?
funFact: Vagn Holmboe's 13 symphonies span decades and represent one of the greatest achievements in 20th-century Danish classical music!

questionText: Aqua's global hit 'Barbie Girl' was released in which year?
funFact: Mattel sued Aqua over 'Barbie Girl' but lost — the U.S. court ruled it was protected parody.
